main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Direct Gaussian Attribute Diffusion (HUMAN-DAD): a conditional diffusion model directly supervised by proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes","Two-stage construction process for proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes (per-scene overfitting and distribution unification)","Attribute-wise loss function for direct supervision","Conditional Gaussian attribute diffusion module with pixel-aligned and SMPL-semantic features"],"problems":["Generalizable monocular 3D human rendering from a single image","Inefficiency and suboptimal convergence of indirect supervision (pixel-level loss) in 3D Gaussian Splatting frameworks","Difficulty in precise error backpropagation due to coupling of 3D Gaussian attributes","Bridging the supervision gap between pixel and attribute domains in 3DGS-based human rendering"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a direct paradigm for training 3D Gaussian Splatting models via attribute-level supervision using proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes, instead of traditional indirect pixel-level supervision.","Introduces a two-stage process (per-scene overfitting and distribution unification) to construct consistent and smoothly distributed proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes.","Designs a conditional diffusion model (HUMAN-DAD) specifically for generating 3D Gaussian attributes from a single image, demonstrating the effectiveness of diffusion models for 3DGS tasks.","Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in generalizable monocular 3D human rendering."]}

methods: Direct Gaussian Attribute Diffusion (HUMAN-DAD): a conditional diffusion model directly supervised by proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes
Two-stage construction process for proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes (per-scene overfitting and distribution unification)
Attribute-wise loss function for direct supervision
Conditional Gaussian attribute diffusion module with pixel-aligned and SMPL-semantic features
problems: Generalizable monocular 3D human rendering from a single image
Inefficiency and suboptimal convergence of indirect supervision (pixel-level loss) in 3D Gaussian Splatting frameworks
Difficulty in precise error backpropagation due to coupling of 3D Gaussian attributes
Bridging the supervision gap between pixel and attribute domains in 3DGS-based human rendering
novelty_claims: Proposes a direct paradigm for training 3D Gaussian Splatting models via attribute-level supervision using proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes, instead of traditional indirect pixel-level supervision.
Introduces a two-stage process (per-scene overfitting and distribution unification) to construct consistent and smoothly distributed proxy-ground-truth 3D Gaussian attributes.
Designs a conditional diffusion model (HUMAN-DAD) specifically for generating 3D Gaussian attributes from a single image, demonstrating the effectiveness of diffusion models for 3DGS tasks.
Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in generalizable monocular 3D human rendering.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["GPS-Gaussian: Generalizable Pixel-Wise 3D Gaussian Splatting","Pixel-wise Gaussian parameter maps defined on 2D source image planes","Feed-forward regression of Gaussian Splatting properties (position, color, scaling, rotation, opacity)","Iterative stereo-matching based depth estimation module","Joint training of Gaussian parameter regression and depth estimation modules","Fully differentiable framework for end-to-end training"],"problems":["Real-time novel view synthesis (NVS) of human characters at high resolution under sparse-view camera settings","Eliminating the need for per-subject or per-frame optimization in 3D Gaussian Splatting","Efficient and generalizable rendering of unseen human characters without fine-tuning or optimization","Accurate depth estimation for lifting 2D parameter maps to 3D space in the presence of self-occlusions"],"datasets":["Large-scale 3D human scan data (exact dataset names not specified in the provided text)","Several datasets for evaluation (exact names not specified in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Rendering speed (FPS)","Image resolution (2K)","Rendering quality (likely PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS, but not explicitly mentioned in the provided text)"],"results":[{"metric":"Rendering speed","value":"exceeding 25 FPS at 2K resolution on a single modern graphics card"}],"novelty_claims":["First generalizable 3D Gaussian Splatting method that regresses Gaussian parameters in a feed-forward manner, eliminating per-subject optimization.","Introduction of pixel-wise Gaussian parameter maps on 2D image planes for 3D Gaussian representation.","Joint, fully differentiable training of depth estimation and Gaussian parameter regression modules.","Real-time, high-resolution (2K) novel view synthesis of unseen human characters without any fine-tuning or optimization."]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Human 3Diffusion: a framework coupling 2D multi-view diffusion models with 3D reconstruction models for avatar creation","Image-conditioned generative 3D Gaussian Splats (3D-GS) reconstruction model leveraging 2D multi-view diffusion priors","A diffusion process that iteratively denoises multi-view images, reconstructs them to 3D-GS, and re-renders to continue the diffusion, ensuring 3D consistency","Joint training of 2D diffusion and 3D-GS models on high-quality human scans"],"problems":["Creating realistic 3D human avatars from a single RGB image","Ensuring 3D consistency in multi-view avatar generation from 2D diffusion models","Overcoming the lack of large-scale 3D human data and ambiguities in monocular 2D view settings","Improving generalization and fidelity of 3D avatar reconstruction, especially in geometry and appearance"],"datasets":["~6000 high-quality human scans (as mentioned in the paper; specific dataset name not provided)"],"metrics":["3D consistency (qualitative and quantitative)","Geometry fidelity","Appearance fidelity"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to tightly couple 2D multi-view diffusion models with explicit 3D reconstruction for avatar creation, leveraging complementary strengths of both approaches","Introduction of a novel image-conditioned generative 3D Gaussian Splats (3D-GS) model that uses 2D diffusion priors for explicit 3D representation","A new diffusion process that incorporates 3D reconstruction at each iteration to refine the 2D reverse sampling trajectory, improving 3D consistency","Demonstrated superior performance and generalization compared to state-of-the-art methods for single-image avatar creation"]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["MPS-NeRF (Multi-Person Skinning NeRF): a generalizable NeRF framework for 3D human rendering using multiview images as conditional input","Canonical NeRF representation: learning a radiance field in a canonical space aligned by a parametric 3D human model (SMPL)","Volume deformation scheme: connecting canonical space with input/target images using volumetric deformation derived from SMPL skinning weights and linear blend skinning","Feed-forward rendering pipeline: synthesizing novel views and poses for unseen persons without per-person optimization","Image feature extraction and radiance prediction networks adapted to the deformation scheme"],"problems":["Generalizable 3D human rendering from sparse multiview still images (not videos)","Novel view synthesis and pose animation for unseen persons (not seen during training)","Overcoming the limitations of person-specific NeRF models that require multiview videos and per-person training"],"datasets":["Human3.6M","Synthetic dataset created from THuman body models"],"metrics":["Rendering quality (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS)","Generalization to unseen persons (qualitative and quantitative evaluation)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to address novel-view and novel-pose rendering for unseen persons using only sparse multiview images (no videos)","Proposes a simple yet effective method combining canonical NeRF and volume deformation based on a parametric human model (SMPL)","Achieves generalization to new people and poses in a feed-forward manner, without per-person optimization","Demonstrates rendering quality on par with or better than previous person-specific models","Provides a strong baseline for future generalizable 3D human rendering research"]}

methods: MPS-NeRF (Multi-Person Skinning NeRF): a generalizable NeRF framework for 3D human rendering using multiview images as conditional input
Canonical NeRF representation: learning a radiance field in a canonical space aligned by a parametric 3D human model (SMPL)
Volume deformation scheme: connecting canonical space with input/target images using volumetric deformation derived from SMPL skinning weights and linear blend skinning
Feed-forward rendering pipeline: synthesizing novel views and poses for unseen persons without per-person optimization
Image feature extraction and radiance prediction networks adapted to the deformation scheme
problems: Generalizable 3D human rendering from sparse multiview still images (not videos)
Novel view synthesis and pose animation for unseen persons (not seen during training)
Overcoming the limitations of person-specific NeRF models that require multiview videos and per-person training
datasets: Synthetic dataset created from THuman body models
metrics: Generalization to unseen persons (qualitative and quantitative evaluation)
novelty_claims: First to address novel-view and novel-pose rendering for unseen persons using only sparse multiview images (no videos)
Proposes a simple yet effective method combining canonical NeRF and volume deformation based on a parametric human model (SMPL)
Achieves generalization to new people and poses in a feed-forward manner, without per-person optimization
Demonstrates rendering quality on par with or better than previous person-specific models
Provides a strong baseline for future generalizable 3D human rendering research

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel Conditional Point Diffusion-Refinement (PDR) paradigm for 3D point cloud completion, combining conditional DDPM and a refinement network.
Identifies conditional DDPM as an effective and efficient model for generating uniform point clouds using mean squared error loss.
Introduces a dual-path network architecture with Denoise and Condition Feature Extraction subnets for both CGNet and RFNet.
Develops new point learning modules: Point Adaptive Deconvolution (PA-Deconv) and Feature Transfer (FT) modules for effective multi-level feature extraction and transfer.
Demonstrates that the RFNet can refine accelerated DDPM outputs, enabling up to 50x faster inference without significant quality loss.
Achieves new state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets for point cloud completion.

problems: Reconstructing fully textured 3D human meshes from single-view (monocular) images
Inferring unknown body shapes, appearances, and clothing details in unobserved regions of the image
Handling the ill-posed nature of 3D reconstruction from 2D images, including ambiguity in depth, surface, and pose
Robustness to unseen appearances and poses due to limited 3D human training data
datasets: CustomHumans (new benchmark created by the authors)

results.value: Superior to state-of-the-art methods on THuman2.0 and CustomHumans benchmarks (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: First to integrate an image-conditioned diffusion model into a 3D mesh reconstruction workflow for single-view textured human reconstruction
Decomposition of the single-view reconstruction problem into generative hallucination and mesh reconstruction subproblems
Efficient training with as few as 500 3D human scans while maintaining robustness and generality
Establishment of a new benchmark (based on CustomHumans) for evaluating textured human reconstruction
Ability to recover full-body textured meshes, including back-view details, from a single image
Significantly faster than previous diffusion-based optimization methods (producing results in under two minutes)
